originalRuleSummary: noGenericOnlyReport passed only when diagnostic.unitCount >= 1 AND (topicLayer.topicRecLabels.length >= 1 OR diagnostic.diagnosedCount >= 1). Evidence logged unitCount and topicRecCount only.
newRuleSummary: noGenericOnlyReport uses reportHasNonGenericSignals(): requires unitCount >= 1, then accepts any of: diagnosed topic units; topic recommendation labels; analysisPreview.needsPracticeLines; executive.topFocusAreasHe; topicLayer.topWeaknessLabels; or substantive diagnostic.unitSummaries (diagnosisLineHe length ≥12 or patternHe ≥8). Evidence records which branch matched (matched field).
whyOriginalFailed: Critical matrix deep scenarios often produced facets where unitCount was positive and diagnosed units existed in unitSummaries, but topicRecLabels stayed empty and diagnosedCount stayed 0 in diagnostic aggregates. The narrow OR gate flagged healthy reports as generic-only.
affectedScenariosNote: Prior failing runs were not persisted with per-scenario facet snapshots in-repo; remediation was validated by making the structured gate align with other facet paths already used elsewhere (e.g. structuredWeaknessHaystack). Re-run critical-deep with noGenericOnlyReport enabled exercises the new rule across all critical_deep_* scenarios.

sampleEvidence.failureModeUnderOldRule: topicRecCount 0, diagnosedCount 0, but unitSummaries contained non-trivial diagnosisLineHe text
sampleEvidence.passUnderNewRule: matched: unitSummariesSubstantive or topFocusAreasHe / needsPracticeLines when topic layer empty

riskAssessment: The new rule is stricter on true empties (still fails when unitCount is 0 or no structural signals exist). It is more accurate than topicRecLabels-only because it matches how the report already surfaces specificity in executive and analysis preview layers. Does not depend on Hebrew copy equality.
recommendedFinalRule: Keep noGenericOnlyReport: true on critical scenarios; implement assertion via reportHasNonGenericSignals (exported from report-assertion-engine.mjs) so future harnesses can reuse the same contract.
